text: I find myself engulfed by an environment of sharp shadows and cool, indifferent neon—a liminal space that feels both clinical and alien. The first image, a foyer or hallway, is deserted and echoes with the hollow sound of my own footsteps. I feel uncertainty, even a slight unease, as if I am waiting for something undefined to happen. The second image, with its glowing screens and abstract words on the wall, confronts me with ideas of progress, technology, and research. Yet, there’s a sense of isolation here, too—a reminder of how detached the fruits of innovation can feel when left uncontextualized. Being here, I am flooded by curiosity but also a subtle existential loneliness, as though I am peering into corridors of knowledge where human presence is only an afterthought.
